error.message:
Local browser act failed. This can happen if the page closes, a modal blocks the page, or the browser session is interrupted. Original error: 

ActActuationError(
    message = TargetClosedError: Page.evaluate: Target page, context or browser has been closed
    metadata = ActMetadata(
        session_id = 019ce53d-7cda-7ee9-8049-975e4bd85127
        act_id = 019ce53d-8dcc-718f-befa-53d3cf752264
        num_steps_executed = 3
        start_time = 2026-03-12 23:29:02.411894 EDT
        end_time = 2026-03-12 23:29:21.343764 EDT
        step_server_times_s = ['2.209', '2.484', '2.783']
        time_worked = 18.9s
        prompt = 'You are helping gather visible public profile information for research and profile review.
    Only inspect information that is already visible in the current browser session.
    Narrate your actions briefly while you move so the operator can follow along.
    Scroll only when needed. Avoid repetitive scrolling. Prefer targeted clicks and concise movement.
    If you complete a stage, move to the next stage immediately instead of revisiting earlier sections repeatedly.
    Primary candidate name: Nguyen Phan Nguyen.
    LINKEDIN STAGE: FOLLOW THESE 30 DETAILED STEPS
    L1. Start on Google search, not LinkedIn directly.
    L2. Search this exact query on Google: Nguyen Phan Nguyen Virginia Tech LinkedIn
    L3. Wait for the Google results page to load.
    L4. Review the first page of results and identify the most relevant LinkedIn profile result.
    L5. Click the most relevant LinkedIn result.
    L6. If a sign-in modal appears and there is a visible close button, dismiss it once.
    L7. If LinkedIn shows a hard authwall with no public profile content, do not try to bypass it.
    L8. Note clearly whether the public profile is blocked or partially visible.
    L9. If the profile is visible, capture the visible URL and top-card header first.
    L10. Capture the headline, location, current employer, and school if shown.
    L11. Capture visible connection/follower counts or audience counts if shown.
    L12. Capture any visible public links or featured external links near the top.
    L13. Read the top summary before scrolling further.
    L14. Scroll to About if present.
    L15. Capture the strongest visible About summary, technical focus, or career interests.
    L16. Scroll to Activity.
    L17. Capture whether public posts, shares, or comments are visible and what themes appear.
    L18. Scroll to Experience.
    L19. If a 'Show all experiences' button is visible, click it once.
    L20. Capture the latest visible role title, company, and dates.
    L21. Capture one or two additional relevant roles, internships, or research positions if visible.
    L22. Capture technologies, outcomes, or impact statements if they are explicitly written.
    L23. Return to the main profile page if you opened an expanded experience view.
    L24. Scroll to Education and capture school, degree, and dates if visible.
    L25. Scroll to Featured and note any visible portfolio, project, or publication links.
    L26. If a clearly relevant public portfolio link is visible, open one, inspect briefly, then return.
    L27. Scroll to Skills and capture the top visible skills.
    L28. Note whether certifications, honors, volunteer work, or organizations are visible.
    L29. Note which desired sections are blocked, hidden, collapsed, or absent.
    L30. End the LinkedIn stage with a concise summary of strongest visible signals, warnings, and missing info.
    GITHUB STAGE: FOLLOW THESE 30 DETAILED STEPS
    G1. Navigate directly to this GitHub URL: https://github.com/ngstephen1
    G2. Wait for the GitHub profile page to load fully.
    G3. Capture the visible profile URL.
    G4. Capture the username exactly as shown.
    G5. Capture the display name exactly as shown.
    G6. Capture the visible bio or tagline.
    G7. Capture follower and following counts if visible.
    G8. Capture location, organization, website, or employer if visible.
    G9. Capture the visible contribution graph summary or contribution count if visible.
    G10. Capture the names of pinned repositories visible on the profile.
    G11. Capture the top visible programming languages or topic tags if visible on the profile.
    G12. Open the Repositories tab if it is not already selected.
    G13. Capture the total repository count if visible.
    G14. Review the top repositories list and choose the strongest repo to inspect first.
    G15. Click the first strong repository.
    G16. Capture repo name, owner, description, and primary language.
    G17. Capture stars, forks, watchers, and last-updated timing if visible.
    G18. Inspect the top of the README and capture whether setup, usage, or demo information is present.
    G19. Inspect the visible file tree and capture whether the repo looks organized into sensible folders/files.
    G20. Note whether tests, CI config, lint config, or package/build files are visible in the root.
    G21. Open the Actions tab if visible and capture whether workflows exist.
    G22. Open the Issues tab if visible and capture whether issues exist and whether activity appears recent.
    G23. Open the Pull requests tab if visible and capture whether PR activity exists.
    G24. Open the Security tab if visible and capture whether advisories or security tooling are shown.
    G25. Return to the repository Code tab or main page and capture any visible badges, releases, or documentation links.
    G26. Return to the main GitHub profile.
    G27. Choose one more strong pinned or notable repository if clearly visible.
    G28. Open that second repository and capture a lighter pass: name, description, language, stars, README quality, and visible engineering signals.
    G29. Return to the main profile and note any overall patterns across repos such as ML, frontend, systems, music tech, or hackathon work.
    G30. End the GitHub stage with a concise summary of the strongest positive engineering signals, weak spots, and missing evidence.
    WEB SEARCH STAGE:
    W1. Open a normal web search context instead of typing into a site-specific search box.
    W2. Use web search for public evidence such as hackathons, conference talks, portfolios, technical blogs, internships, or awards.
    W3. Search this exact query: Nguyen Phan Nguyen Virginia Tech
    W4. Search this exact query: Nguyen Phan Nguyen Software Engineer
    W9. Open only clearly relevant public results and summarize only the strongest visible findings.
    W10. Avoid typing web queries into GitHub or LinkedIn site search fields.
    FINAL REPORT:
    F1. End with a concise report of the strongest positive signals, any warnings, and any missing information.
    F2. Once all requested stages are complete, stop and return control immediately. Do not continue exploring.'
    )
)

Please consider providing feedback: https://amazonexteu.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_bd8dHa7Em6kNkMe
